hypothesis: If local iron coordination environments in proteins are encoded as graphs and paired with accurately digitized, standardized Fe K-edge XAS spectra, then machine-learning models will both predict 100-point XANES/EXAFS spectra from structure with low error and recover coordination number and mean nearest-neighbor distance from spectra above baseline, because Fe K-edge spectral features are determined by the local atomic geometry and are preserved by the curation pipeline.
